evidence_quotes.quote: Consider adding a clause against making purchased games unplayable for those who have bought them.
Please, add protections to ensure consumers maintain access to games they have purchased, even if DRM or platform support ends.
limit user privacy and extract data from us as if we were nothing more than things to be used and discarded, not customers to be served.

Please, introduce regulation ensuring that payment processors cannot deny service to legal businesses or individuals wishing to purchase legal products, and recognize their function as critical infrastructure for the well being of all citizens, rich or poor, all of us are at risk of their censorship.
They should not be able to coerce or otherwise bully anyone, companies or people, into censoring themselves.

paragraphs.text: Hi, first of all, allow me to thank you for proposing this resolution, in the modern age, it is critical we ensure citizen and consumer safety in an increasingly dangerous political climate. I absolutely love this resolution, but I propose one thing: Consider adding a clause against making purchased games unplayable for those who have bought them. If purchasing an item does not equal owning it, then the industry creating said item (video games in this case) will rapidly descend into increasingly predatory marketing. It should be noted this clause would send forth a message to all other industries as well, the message would be clear. "Do not abuse our people your customers, or we will protect them." Which would surely improve industry standards in all sorts of unique ways. Please, add protections to ensure consumers maintain access to games they have purchased, even if DRM or platform support ends. This isn't just about games, its about setting standards and making an example, not to mention protecting our digital art and culture from corporate decay. Another clause I kindly request you consider is one preventing payment transaction companies, such as VISA or MasterCard, from banning its users from buying legal products. These companies have a chokehold on much of the world's finace related trafic, and they should be treated not as businesses, but as necessary infrastructure such as the electricity or water industries in the various respected nations. They should not be able to coerce or otherwise bully anyone, companies or people, into censoring themselves. That is tyrannical and an insult to the rights we, europeans, hold so dearly to our hearts, rights millions have struggled for in the past. Please, introduce regulation ensuring that payment processors cannot deny service to legal businesses or individuals wishing to purchase legal products, and recognize their function as critical infrastructure for the well being of all citizens, rich or poor, all of us are at risk of their censorship. Please, I beg of you as an honored citizen of your organization, protect us. I have never felt more anxious in my life - with companies closing in with censorship, silencing things I enjoy and judging with corporate greed, and even various organizations taking steps to limit user privacy and extract data from us as if we were nothing more than things to be used and discarded, not customers to be served. I have faith in democracy, and thus in you and your system. Please do not fail me, as the EU is the last uncorrupted bastion of rights and democracy in an increasingly twisted world. I apologize for gramatical mishaps, I am not a native english speaker, but found it more fitting to write in english rather than czech. Thank you dearly. From a worried yet hopeful EU citizen.
